title: How to sum every nth row
task: Add up every 2nd, 3rd, or nth value in a column — common with repeating blocks of data.

excel.note: Change the 3 to your n. Anchoring on ROW(B2) makes the first row count as position 0.

explanation: ROW(B2:B7)-ROW(B2) numbers the rows 0,1,2,3,4,5 regardless of where the range starts; MOD(...,3)=0 is true for positions 0 and 3 — every 3rd row. SUMPRODUCT multiplies those TRUE/FALSE values (1/0) by the numbers and sums: 10 + 40 = 50. Every-other-row is the same formula with 2, and MOD(...,3)=1 shifts the starting offset.
